questions.question: What is the primary assumption of random sampling that ensures the sample mean is an unbiased estimator of the population mean?

A researcher is conducting a study to investigate the relationship between two variables. The study involves collecting data from a sample of 100 participants, and the sample is selected using a simple random sampling method. What type of bias is this study susceptible to?
questions.hint: Bias type in simple random sampling

questions.question: Suppose a researcher conducts a survey to measure the average height of a population. The survey results show a sample mean height of 175 cm, with a standard deviation of 5 cm. If the researcher takes another sample of 100 participants, what can be said about the sample mean height in this new sample?
